item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is both horrifying and funny. I cracked up when I got to "pursue legal and/or illegal actions." Those poor creatures, though. I don't think whatever entity is doing this is causing intentional harm, which makes it kind of sad…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Darn, I was hoping for a pun along the lines of "MAKE A WISH, FOUNDATION!".

The "anomalous items that usually hurt or kill their users manufactured by quirky extradimensional beings" market is getting pretty crowded these days. Did the Factory lose an anti-trust lawsuit or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's one thing I don't understand. It says "GOI-4998 is an organization that haphazardly generates unpredictably dangerous anomalies for no shown monetary gain," but it doesn't say that the dogs are in any way dangerous or anomalous. Just horribly deformed. The phone calls and boxes also seem normal. The only anomalies are in their method of transmission, and whatever process apparently makes the dogs.

Also, they should totally DNA test those dogs. I bet there's tons of filler pet in them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
what if want cat or lizard

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All my dumb friends would fall for this dumb scam. This is bizarre and funny and concerning. I do want DOG. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Object Classes are based on containment difficulty, this is uncontained and can definitely cause a massive stir-up if DOG gets into the public unattended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one was funny. It's just one of those "Wow, what the hell?" kind of SCPs. However, it doesn't really seem like a Keter to me. From they way you described the creatures that are sent, it sounds like they can't exactly do much in terms of damage. That said, I don't think it's entirely safe either. I think it would fit better as an Euclid type.

yay

Well this passed Santa quite fast in rating. Thanks for +50!

item.title: Noice.
item.content:encoded: When they got to "WHAT KIND OF CHEESE YOU LIKE?" I just had to laugh. Take your upvote dammit.

Would you look at that!

Both my first and second SCPs getting +40 almost at the same time, neat. Thanks everyone, and this is definitely not the last you'll see of MAKE WISH FOUNDATON.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just the name alone "WANT DOG" told me that this is going to be good, and it was.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The POI is just so profoundly weird, cheerful, funny, and unsettling all the time that I can't not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved this line as well. :)

item.title: Upvote!
item.content:encoded:
So, I agree those issues should be fixed, but I upvoted this because (A) the article is creative and fun and (B) I'm so tired of clinical tone being critiqued without useful suggestions to fix it.

So I'm going to provide some suggestions. Take them or leave them. I'm going to give your version followed by a suggested version. Now, given that I've been pinged a lot of clinical tone myself, maybe take with a big grain of salt, but I figure it can't hurt.

+ show block
– hide block

Before I start, I'll note in passing that it's a little odd that we learn about the video the researcher got and but not the SCP-4157-2 instance itself.

Anyhoo, on to the suggested edits…

—

This is to occur until the SCP-4157-2 instance following the call manifests.

Individuals are to be kept away until the SCP-4157-2 instance following the call manifests.

—

Current investigations into GOI-4998 are underway, although tracking of the number on SCP-4157-1 yields no results to viable locations for a front organization for GOI-4998.

Current investigations into GOI-4998 are underway, although tracking the number on SCP-4157-1 instances have yielded no viable locations for front organizations for GOI-4998.

—

SCP-4157 events have only been shown to occur in North America, with SCP-4157-1 instances only appearing in areas and at times where no video surveillance and/or human viewing is currently present.

SCP-4157 events have only been known to occur in North America, with SCP-4157-1 instances only appearing in areas and at times without video surveillance or human witnesses.

—

Most SCP-4157-2 instances are hostile and sporadic, but do not survive for long periods of time after their initial appearance due to all recorded specimens showing some degree of distortion of organ, muscle, and bone structure compared to normal canines, resulting in major organ failure within the first day of manifestation in 85.6% of SCP-4157-2 instances. 44.7% of the previous percentage are SCP-4157-2 instances that are deceased upon arrival to the individual's household.

Living SCP-4157-2 instances are hostile, but do not survive for long due to distortion of organ, muscle, and bone structure compared to normal canines, resulting in major organ failure within the first day of arrival at the delivery residence in 85.6% of cases. 44.7% of the SCP-4157-2 instances that die on the day of arrival are deceased even before opening the box.

—


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

I disagree with mayoculpa about the description of the GoI. It's short, but it gets the point across, so I have no suggested edit there.

Overall, great SCP, and don't let the dado comparisons get you down. I'd love to see more from the MAKE WISH FOUNDATION. Merry Christmas, and have another +1! :)

-Kirt

item.title: Re: Grammatical Error
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: Grammatical Error
item.content:encoded:
"The body of the SCP-4157-2 instance is to then to be disposed of along with any other objects found within its originating box."

Did you mean is to then be?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted with extreme prejudice.

There are clearly a few issues with tone and grammar, but the concept and execution is absolutely more than enough to make up for it. Surreal SCPs that manage to be both hilarious and disturbing are my personal favorites.

Keep up the good work! The equal-parts-funny-and-disturbing vibe this entry has is great. Also, don't worry about MAKE WISH FOUNDATION (great name btw) being too similar to dado; the fact that they apparently just make things without request, and how random it is, separates it enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
POI-4998: You heard me.

I freaking lost it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Twist: PoI is made of cat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good. I feel like getting DOG.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It is quite unsettling. I am reminded of a mix of dado and that one SCP that made horses sound disturbing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've attempted to fix most of these, but the dado comparisons are just something I'm gonna have to live with on this I guess. I mean, I knew that it was going to happen, but seeing how bad it is (with 3 comments so far referencing him in some way), I might try to rework this into a dado article possibly, but I honestly don't think dado is compatible with the slightly unnerving tone I'm trying to go for here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are several awkward lines in the technical parts of this, that could use some reworking. For example, "…instances only appearing in areas and at times where no video surveillance and/or human viewing is currently present at said location of appearance" could certainly be trimmed down. Describing the questions as "random and bizarre" seems too casual in tone to me, especially since not all the questions in the included phone transcript were necessarily bizarre, only seemingly unrelated to the selection of a dog. On the other hand, I tend to be hyper aware of words that reached excessive popularity and misuse in internet culture, so "random" might be setting me off more than it would another reader. Lines such as "sporadically hostile to anyone/anything within their surroundings" seem too vague. Do individual instances show behavior that cycles between periods of hostility and non-agression, or are some instances hostile, while others are not? Same with "high to low distortion of organ, muscle, and bone structure." May I suggest "some degree of distortion" in place of "high to low?"

I'd love to see this play off of the inevitable dado comparisons in some way. Whether or not your idea came first, dado has enough of an established presence that folks are gonna have some kind of opinion about that. Why not try an in-universe relationship between these entities, whether that is cooperative, competitive, hostile, or what have you?

item.title: dado?
item.content:encoded: I never thought I would be asking myself and my friends "hey how wide is Jesus?" +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really wanted to like it, but I'm gonna downvote.

And here's why.
Close crits

There are occasional issues with clinical tone throughout the article. For example:

SCP-4157-2 instances are aggressive and sporadic, and will act with hostility towards anyone/anything in their surroundings upon arrival.

could have been phrased much more clearly. And the footnote about the GoI sounds, frankly, as though it were written by the GoI. It could be presented as a quotation from some source document, or set up with additional support elsewhere as a memetic effect, but as written it comes off as kind of sloppy.

The video log at the end was pretty much exactly what I was expecting based on the lead-in. There was definitely horror and there was definitely comedy, but for me they never really found a balance point that stuck. Instead of being creeped out or amused, in the end I was just sad that this delusional anomalous artist and/or businessperson is out there serially hurting dogs (and possibly people) because they've convinced themselves that that's a good thing to do.

item.title: Re: what cheese
item.content:encoded: It feels very dado-esque, but you may just be a victim of bad timing in that regard.

item.title: Re: what cheese
item.content:encoded: Fun fact, I actually wrote the OG draft for this before the first dado article was posted about a year ago before I had an account.

item.title: what cheese
item.content:encoded:
I instantly thought this was another dano SCP and I was really happy

but holy shit this is even better

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hi, I upvoted this before you redid it(I think) and now that its even better, you should stick with this even in the case that this gets deleted which dosent seem to be the case. So good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This line made me laugh too much.

+ show block
– hide block

POI-4998: Great! What is your favorite method of murdering a wealthy married couple in their home and then subsequently robbing them of their valuables?


Take your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Want Dog?

I posted this before, didn't like it, and deleted it. Now that I've gotten some helpful crit from Reverend Fox, I finally feel that this is decent enough to post and have it stay up. So yeah, I hope this doesn't get downvoted into oblivion and I have to delete it again and scrap the idea for a while. And I am sorry if this breaks some rule (I'm pretty sure it doesn't however).

Also, just so I don't have to keep repeating myself, I actually wrote the original draft for this before the first dado article was posted from what I can tell. I have changed it a bunch to differentiate, and it was a lot more similar to dado before about 200 rewrites.

Like this? Go to my Author Page!
